cases.hypothesis: DEFAULT_STOPWORDS includes 'what', 'is', 'a' - all three stopwords. meaningfulTokens() reduces the query to just 'wallet'. The fallback `kept.length > 0 ? kept : toks` keeps just 'wallet'. The pin map DOES have 'wallet' -> standard/wallets/how-it-works, so the pin would fire if normalized query equals 'wallet' - but the normalized query is 'what is a wallet', not 'wallet'. The pin lookup checks normalized (and corrected normalized), not the post-stopword tokens. So the pin misses, and the bare 'wallet' search competes against dozens of wallet pages (wallets/comparison, wallets/v4, wallets/v5, wallet-apps/, etc.).

cases.q: how do I use get methods
cases.hypothesis: Stopwords strip 'how', 'do', 'i' (note 'do' is NOT in DEFAULT_STOPWORDS per code - rechecking: DEFAULT_STOPWORDS does NOT include 'do' explicitly, only 'does'; 'i' IS in the list; 'how' IS in the list; 'use' is in DEFAULT_STOPWORDS). After stripping: 'do get methods'. The 'use' stripping kills user intent. The normalized query is 'how do i use get methods' which doesn't match any pin key ('get method' / 'get methods' exist as pins to the same URL, but only exact normalized-query equality is checked). Then 'do get methods' as a search competes against many pages with 'get' in URL (toncenter has hundreds of get-* URLs).

cases.q: how do you call a contract
cases.hypothesis: Stopwords strip 'how', 'you', 'a' (and 'do' is in BASELINE not DEFAULT). 'call' is preserved (DEFAULT_STOPWORDS deliberately omits call/send/use/get). Resulting tokens: 'do call contract'. The intent is calling a contract method, but the bare lexical search hits hundreds of pages mentioning 'contract' and 'call' (every smart contract page). No pin for 'call a contract'. The canonical 'How to interact with wallets' page doesn't even contain 'call' prominently in title — title-include scoring won't fire.

Porter stemming maps 'running' -> 'run' and 'node' -> 'node'. The tokenizer with stemming=true means the indexed token is 'run'. This stems 'run-validator', 'run a node', 'run a node with MyTonCtrl', plus tons of `run-method` URLs (run-get-method, run-get-method-standard, runtime, runs etc.). Result: setup-mytonctrl competes against nodes/cpp/run-validator (which has 'Run' in title literal), nodes/rust/quick-start ('Rust node quick start'), and many toncenter/v2/run-method/* and apiv2/run-get-method URLs that all 'run' stems to. Title heuristic ties between multiple node-running pages.

Title is just 'Overview' - the same title as DOZENS of other pages (api/overview, jettons/overview, nft/overview, tolk/overview, etc.). The page title content stored in Orama is 'Overview' which doesn't include 'messages'. title.includes('message') fails. The exactTitleWeight=3 only matches when title.trim() === queryNorm — title is 'overview' which never equals 'messages'. URL contains 'messages' so urlWeight=1 fires. But many other long pages discuss messages heavily (internal, external-in, external-out, deploy, modes, ordinary-tx) and have 'messages' in their breadcrumbs. The canonical /messages/overview is buried.

Canonical title is 'Token overview' (singular). Porter stems 'tokens' -> 'token' so the token field matches via stemming. No pin for 'tokens' or 'token'. exactTitleWeight=3 won't fire ('token overview' != 'tokens'). Competes against many long token-heavy pages: jettons/overview, nft/overview, nft/how-it-works, jettons/how-it-works, plus toncenter pages like get-token-data. Long pages with high BM25 saturated with 'token' will win.

TVM opcode identifier. Porter stemming on 'muldivmodr' won't help (no English root). The tvm/instructions page IS long and term-dense — likely contains MULDIVMODR if it's a standard opcode, but in a hugely long page (multi-KB). However, the only place it can appear is the long instructions page; if absent from the curated '#Code symbols' synthetic row (the comment in the pipeline notes 343 auto-mined Code symbols rows exist but DON'T earn structHit bonus), then the page must be found purely via content indexing. With tolerance:1 fuzzy, a long-tail opcode token may collide with unrelated word stems. If MULDIVMODR doesn't appear literally on the canonical page, the search returns wrong results entirely.

Composed identifier (c5 register concept). Unlikely to appear literally on the registers page. Underscores in the query are treated by tokenize() as a single token (regex /\s+/ doesn't split on underscore), so the token is 'register_num_for_c5'. Orama with tolerance:1 needs exact substring; this won't fuzzy-match. The token has no curated spell mapping. Result: zero matches on the right page; pipeline returns whatever Orama happens to find via fuzzy fallback (likely nothing useful). twoPassGroups returns empty, and ranking degrades to whatever pin/spell catches — none here.

Period in identifier — tokenize() splits only on /\s+/, so 'blockchain.config' is ONE token. Orama indexes tokens after its tokenizer (English with stemming) which by default DOES split on punctuation, so the index has 'blockchain' and 'config' separately, but our query passes 'blockchain.config' as a single search term. Orama's BM25 search with this single weird token finds nothing exact. Tolerance:1 might fuzzy match 'blockchain' or 'config' alone but the combined token confuses the matcher. Pipeline returns degraded results — wrong pages or empty.

Single navigational token 'validators'. No pin entry. Porter stems to 'valid' (Porter is aggressive: 'validator' -> 'valid' in some implementations, though Porter2/Snowball preserves it). With stemming=true, 'validators' -> 'valid' might collide with 'validate', 'validation', 'valid' from many pages. Even if it stems correctly to 'validat', mytonctrl/validator title 'Validator' and run-validator title 'Run a validator' both compete. exactTitleWeight on 'validators' (plural query) won't equal 'validator' (singular title). titlePrefixWeight=0. Hard to predict which validator page wins; canonical run-validator likely loses to mytonctrl/validator (shorter title).

Stopwords strip 'how', 'to'. Tokens: 'send ton'. 'send' is preserved (deliberately not stopword'd). 'ton' is on EVERY page in the corpus (it's literally the name of the blockchain). Result: 'send' + 'ton' matches everything. The canonical short 'Send Toncoin' page (note: title 'Send Toncoin', not 'Send TON') has to compete via titleWeight - but the query token 'ton' is a substring of 'toncoin' so title.includes('ton') fires. Many long pages have higher BM25 density. exactTitleWeight won't fire ('send toncoin' != 'send ton'). Pin for 'send ton' missing.

The canonical page title is 'TON wallet mnemonics' - doesn't contain 'key' or 'derivation' in title. The user query terms have no overlap with the title and possibly weak overlap with body. Meanwhile 'derive' appears in /addresses/derive (title 'Deriving addresses') which is a totally different concept. Synonym gap: the user's mental model uses cryptographic vocabulary that's not in the page's curated title or keywords. titleWeight won't fire on either token for the mnemonics page; BM25 must do the work alone but the page may not even contain 'derivation' literally.

Porter stems 'running' -> 'run' and 'tests' -> 'test'. Many run-* URLs (run-method/run-get-method, run-validator) match the 'run' stem. The 'test' stem matches testing pages but also tact tests, security audits, validator testing. Canonical blueprint/testing/overview title is just 'Overview' (title_ambiguity AND stem collision compound). titleWeight on 'overview' doesn't catch query 'running tests' either way. Long reference pages with both terms (blueprint/develop, blueprint/coverage, blueprint/testing/reference) will outscore.

ACCEPT is the TVM opcode external-in messages must invoke to pay for gas. The canonical doc is 'Incoming external messages'. Stopwords list does not include 'opcode', but 'accept' is a common English word so BM25 will float verbose tvm/instructions pages above the short canonical page. structHitWeight only fires on `#Keywords` rows (line 441), and 'accept' likely is not curated as a keyword on the external-in page; the bm25Weight=2.5 blend rewards long term-dense reference pages.

load_msg_addr is the FunC stdlib function for parsing a MsgAddress slice. The Porter english stemmer treats it as one token containing underscores; tokenize() splits only on whitespace (line 265) so 'load_msg_addr' stays intact. The canonical 'Reading and writing addresses' page very likely contains the identifier only inside code-block content (a non-title `#Code symbols` row); per line 441 those rows DON'T contribute structHitWeight, so the FunC stdlib page wins on BM25 length/density.

store_uint is the primary builder primitive across FunC and Tolk. The query is a single underscored identifier with no whitespace, so meaningfulTokens() yields the literal string. The 'Cells' canonical page's title does not contain 'store_uint'; it appears only in code examples (likely a `#Code symbols` row), which per line 441 contributes recall via Orama but no re-rank bonus. The FunC stdlib (lengthy) wins on BM25.

throw_unless is the canonical FunC exception primitive; users searching it want the exit-codes documentation. Title is 'Exit codes' which shares zero tokens with the query, so titleWeight contributes nothing (line 410). The query stems on a non-English token, BM25 likely puts the FunC stdlib reference (long, term-dense, contains the identifier in prose) first; the short canonical short page is outranked by length-dense pages — exactly the failure mode bm25Weight=2.5 is calibrated for but cannot fully fix (line 220 comment: bm25=3 'regresses 2 curated queries').

begin_cell() is the canonical FunC/Tolk constructor for a Builder. Stemming applies to 'begin' (English) but 'cell' is also a stop-candidate for English Porter (no, it isn't). The query has an underscore so it's one token; the Cells canonical page mentions it in code only. The FunC stdlib page (very long, with hundreds of identifiers and prose) will dominate via BM25 length/density.

Single token 'slice' is a TVM type. Many pages reference slices in titles or headings ('Builders and Slices', Tolk 'Cells, slices, builders', and dozens of FunC/Tolk pages). exactTitleWeight (line 417) compares normalized title equality; no page title equals 'slice'. titleWeight integer points (line 410) award the bonus to every page that contains 'slice' in title, so 'Builders and Slices' and 'Cells, slices, builders' tie and the tiebreak resolves by BM25 then crawl order (line 474). The canonical 'Cells' page (which introduces slice as a primitive) loses.

'Builder' is a fundamental TVM type but there is no dedicated 'Builder' page; it lives inside 'Cells'. exactTitleWeight (line 417) tests title === query; 'Cells' !== 'builder' so no exact-title bonus. Meanwhile 'Builders and Slices' (TVM page) has title token match — titleWeight fires there. The canonical Cells page has no 'builder' token in its title or url, so all integer-point weights miss and BM25 (line 431) is the only differentiator, which favors longer pages.

TonClient4 is a specific SDK client class from @ton/ton. Lowercased to 'tonclient4', Porter stemmer leaves it intact. No page title contains it; it may appear only inside fenced code blocks (a `#Code symbols` row), which per the line 441 comment 'still aid recall/candidacy via Orama; they just don't earn a re-rank bonus.' The numeric '4' suffix tightly couples with the identifier making fuzzy tolerance:1 (line 308) unable to discover relevant text. Pipeline will return some toncenter v2/v3 page with the most term density and miss the right one.

'internal_transfer' is the on-chain jetton wallet message opcode/handler name. Single underscored token, no page title contains it. The Jetton api page is short with the canonical messages. Long pages like 'On-chain jetton processing' or 'Jetton implementations comparison' contain the literal in code examples and will be heavily favored by the bm25Weight=2.5 blend. structHitWeight (line 441) bypasses `#Code symbols` rows so the canonical page can't recover via curated bonus.

TEP-62 is the NFT standard. Same failure mode as TEP-74: tokenized as 'tep-62', not in the pin map (only 'nft', 'nfts' are pinned, line 86-87), title 'NFT: Non-fungible token' has no 'tep' or '62' token, so the integer re-rank weights all miss. BM25 may surface 'NFT implementations comparison' (longer, contains 'TEP-62' in prose) instead of the canonical overview. The pipeline cannot bridge the standard-number identifier to the named concept.

GETGAS is a TVM opcode for reading remaining gas. After lowercasing it's 'getgas'. The canonical page title is 'Gas'; titleWeight (line 410) checks title.includes('getgas') which fails. tokenize splits on whitespace only (line 265), so 'getgas' is a single token. exactTitleWeight checks title === 'getgas' which fails. The instructions reference page (very long) contains the opcode literal and via BM25 (line 431) outranks the short canonical page. The pipeline's #Code symbols curation is bypassed for re-rank (line 441 comment).

Two-word navigational query for the canonical explorers landing page. Pin map (line 81) does not include 'blockchain explorer' or 'explorer'. titleWeight (line 410): title 'Overview' contains neither 'blockchain' nor 'explorer'. Many pages have 'blockchain' in breadcrumbs (every /blockchain-basics/* page) and the 'Telegram Open Network (TON) Blockchain Specification' whitepaper page has high BM25 weight for 'blockchain'. The pipeline picks a long whitepaper page instead of the short navigational overview.

User says 'minter' (the jetton master contract that mints). Porter stems 'minter' to 'minter' or 'mint' depending on configuration. If the index has 'minter' as a token in 'How to mint new jettons' content but the title uses 'mint', then after stemming both reduce to 'mint'. The titleWeight scorer (line 410) however uses raw title.includes('minter'), which fails for title 'How to mint new jettons' (no 'minter' substring). So even though Orama BM25 retrieves the right candidate, the integer re-rank weight doesn't fire, and BM25 (line 431) alone may pick a longer page mentioning 'minter' (like jettons api or comparison) instead. This exposes the stem/raw mismatch between BM25 retrieval (stemmed) and re-rank scoring (raw substring).

cases.q: how do i make my coins not move until later
cases.hypothesis: After meaningfulTokens strips stopwords (how, do, i, my, not, until), the remaining tokens 'make coins move later' share no lexical overlap with the canonical 'Lockup wallet' page's title or URL. The pipeline only has one full-text 'content' field and no semantic expansion; runRankedSearch's score() relies on token presence in title/breadcrumbs/url, none of which contain 'coins', 'move', or 'later'. The lockup page won't even surface from twoPassGroups because Orama's BM25 over stemmed terms won't match without 'lockup' or 'vest' in the query.

User wants the Account status page (which covers 'frozen' state), but query uses 'freeze' (verb) and 'balance'. Porter stemmer maps 'freeze'->'freez' but the canonical page likely uses 'frozen'->'frozen' (irregular). The score() function in runRankedSearch awards titleWeight/urlWeight only on substring inclusion; neither title 'Account status' nor URL /primitives/status contains 'freeze' or 'balance', so the page can only ride BM25, which will be dominated by long reference pages that mention 'freeze' more often.

cases.q: what does it cost to send a message
cases.hypothesis: DEFAULT_STOPWORDS strips what/does/it/to/a, leaving only 'cost', 'send', 'message'. The 'Transaction fees' canonical page has no 'cost' or 'send' or 'message' in title/url. Meanwhile 'Send message' (/primitives/actions/send) and many message-handling pages contain 'send' and 'message' verbatim, racking up titleWeight=2 + urlWeight=1 per token. The query never tokenizes to 'fees' or 'fee' so the canonical page only competes via BM25, which is bm25Weight=2.5 max — easily outweighed by 3-6 integer points from spurious title/url matches on /primitives/actions/send.

DEFAULT_PINS has no entry for the bare query 'ton', yet hundreds of pages contain the substring 'ton' in title/url/breadcrumbs. Without a pin, the score() function gives every page with 'ton' anywhere haystackWeight+titleWeight+urlWeight points. Tied pages resolve to crawl order via the final 'a.i - b.i' tiebreaker in runRankedSearch's sort, so the result is effectively arbitrary among hundreds of contenders. Start Here cannot win without an exact-title or pin boost.

'docs' is not in DEFAULT_PINS and no canonical page has 'docs' in its title or URL. After meaningfulTokens (length>1 and not stopword) only 'docs' remains. The Porter stem of 'docs' is 'doc' which may match 'documentation' on style-guide pages. The pipeline lacks any heuristic to map 'docs' to the landing page; runRankedSearch will surface random pages whose breadcrumbs happen to contain 'docs'.

DEFAULT_PINS has 'start here' but NOT 'getting started'. The canonical /overview/start-here has title 'Start here' which doesn't contain 'getting' or 'started'. Pages like /applications/appkit/get-started/get-started ('Get started with AppKit') and /applications/ton-connect/get-started ('Get started with TON Connect') and the ecosystem 'Getting started' (telegram-ui) all match both query tokens in title/url/breadcrumbs. The score() function gives each ~6 integer points; the canonical page cannot win without a pin.

Stopwords strip 'to', leaving 'transfer money another wallet'. 'money' is not in the corpus terminology (TON uses 'Toncoin'/'jettons'). The user's right answer is 'Send Toncoin', but score() rewards literal token matches: /blockchain-basics/standard/tokens/jettons/transfer ('How to transfer jettons') and various NFT/wallet 'transfer' pages contain 'transfer' and 'wallet' in title/url. The pipeline lacks any synonym mapping ('money'->Toncoin), so the canonical 'Send Toncoin' page (which doesn't contain 'transfer' or 'money') loses on lexical overlap.

cases.q: code that runs when contract receives money
cases.hypothesis: User describes an internal message handler / receive function in plain English. Stopwords strip 'that', 'when', leaving 'code runs contract receives money'. None of those tokens appear in the canonical 'Handling messages' title or URL. 'money' is alien vocabulary. score() reads token presence in title/haystack/url only; ranks /blockchain-basics/payments/* pages high because their URLs contain 'payment' which doesn't stem to 'money'. Net: Tolk message-handling page gets near-zero rerank bonus despite being the right answer.

cases.q: i deployed and now it says inactive
cases.hypothesis: Natural troubleshooting phrasing. Stopwords strip 'i', 'and', 'now', 'it', leaving 'deployed says inactive'. The canonical Account status page covers uninit/active/frozen but uses 'uninit' not 'inactive'. 'inactive' has no stem-match to any canonical term; Porter stemming reduces 'inactive'->'inact' which has zero collisions in the corpus. The score() function will float deploy-related pages (/primitives/messages/deploy, /blueprint/deploy) on the 'deployed' token, drowning the status page.

At least 20 pages in the corpus have the title literally 'Overview' (e.g., /applications/api/overview, /blockchain-basics/primitives/messages/overview, /blockchain-basics/whitepapers/overview, /blockchain-basics/primitives/actions/overview, etc.). When the user types 'overview', exactTitleWeight=3 fires for ALL of them simultaneously. Tiebreaker is bm25Weight*bm25 then crawl order. The Start here page doesn't even have 'overview' in its title — it cannot win this race even though /overview/* URL prefix implies it should be the landing page.

Porter stems 'address'->'address' and 'addresses'->'address' so stem matches both. Pages with 'address' in title: 'Address' (tolk/types/address), 'Addresses overview', 'Internal address formats', 'Reading and writing addresses', 'Deriving addresses', 'Addresses workflow', and a dozen API endpoint pages like get-address-balance, get-address-information, etc. exactTitleWeight=3 fires for tolk/types/address (title 'Address' === 'address'), promoting that page over the conceptual overview. score() heuristic + bm25 cannot reliably prefer the conceptual primitives overview.

0x178d4519 is the jetton transfer opcode. The query token '0x178d4519' is a hex literal. Pages document opcodes in their #Code symbols curated row per the comment in score(): 'the 343 auto-mined #Code symbols rows ... do NOT earn a re-rank bonus' (the filter is endsWith('#Keywords') only). So even if Orama matches this in #Code symbols, structHitWeight is bypassed. titleWeight/urlWeight cannot match (0x178d4519 not in any URL or title). The page rides on raw BM25 only, which is normalized to [0, 2.5] — easily lost behind keyword-spam pages.

Exit code 13 is 'Out of gas'. Stopwords don't strip these tokens. Title 'Exit codes' contains 'codes' not 'phase'. /primitives/phases 'Execution phases' will rank higher because it contains both 'compute' and 'phase' verbatim. The canonical exit-codes page contains the number 13 somewhere in body, but not in title/url. No mechanism in score() boosts pages that contain numeric identifiers; the bm25 blend caps at 2.5 vs the +3-4 integer score that /phases gets from title/url matches on 'phase'.

SENDRAWMSG is a TVM instruction relevant to send modes. Tokenize lowercases to 'sendrawmsg'. The canonical 'Sending modes' page may have this in body but its #Code symbols row (where TVM instructions live per the structHit comment) won't earn structHitWeight=2 because the filter is endsWith('#Keywords'). Pages like /tvm/instructions ('Instructions') contain 'sendrawmsg' more densely; bm25Weight=2.5 cannot overcome the title/url advantage that the /tvm/instructions page has (none for /messages/modes which only has 'modes' to match the empty query).

'dictinary' -> 'dictionary': missing 'o', extra deletion makes this edit distance 2 from 'dictionary', but Orama's tokenizer applies Porter stemming first. After stemming, 'dictinary'->'dictinari' and 'dictionaries'->'dictionari' — these differ by 'a' vs 'a' positioning, may not match. DEFAULT_SPELL has no entry for 'dictinary'. tolerance:1 in twoPassGroups handles only 1-edit fuzzy. Result: no recall for the right page.

'valdiator' for 'validator' is a transposition (d-i swap), edit distance 2 from 'validator'. DEFAULT_SPELL has 'validater'->'validator' but NOT 'valdiator'. Orama tolerance:1 can't fix a 2-edit typo. The two-pass twoPassGroups returns essentially nothing for 'valdiator'. With no recall, even pins and rerank can't help — the right page never enters the candidate set. score() never executes for the validator page.

'concensus' vs 'consensus' is a 1-edit typo (extra 'c'), so Orama tolerance:1 should fuzzy-match. But the Porter stemmer runs at INDEX time on documents and at QUERY time. The stemmer may not normalize misspellings consistently. DEFAULT_SPELL has no 'concensus' entry. If tokenizer treats 'concensus' as a known stem-target, it still won't surface 'consensus' unless tolerance:1 catches it after stemming. The 'Catchain consensus' page may rank below /primitives/consensus/catchain-visualizer if both surface.

DEFAULT_PINS has 'get method' and 'get methods' (singular/plural), BUT the pin only fires when the NORMALIZED query exactly equals the key. normalizeQuery('how to use get methods') = 'how to use get methods', which is not in pins. After meaningfulTokens strips 'how', 'to', the remaining 'use get methods' doesn't match the pin map either (pin lookup uses the raw normalized string, see pinKeys = [normalized]). So the pin is bypassed and the page competes on lexical match — 'use' is a load-bearing dev term retained but its presence in many pages dilutes the signal.

The pipeline comment literally calls this query out as the canonical stopword failure case: 'Stripping them from the query (not the index) removes the noise that made e.g. "how to deploy a contract" match "a"-heavy opcode pages'. Even WITH stopword stripping, 'deploy contract' is too ambiguous — DEFAULT_PINS has 'smart contract' but the user typed 'a contract' without 'smart'. The pin doesn't fire. score() reads many pages with 'deploy' in title/url; the Introduction page (title 'Introduction to smart contract development') ranks low because 'deploy' isn't in its title/URL.

DEFAULT_STOPWORDS explicitly preserves 'use' (it's in the comment: 'NO get/set/use/call/send/run' exclusions). So 'use' survives as a token. But meaningfulTokens filters t.length > 1, and 'use' is length 3, so it stays. Bare query 'use' matches countless pages — every 'How to use' titled page in the corpus (dozens). exactTitleWeight=3 doesn't fire (no page titled just 'Use'). Pipeline returns essentially random crawl-order results. There's no pin for 'use' and no notion of 'beginner intent'.
